stillOpen.reason: GATEWAY_RPC_DEFINITIONS freezes 19 methods; the runtime dispatch in packages/server/src/gateway.js handles additional canonical (non-legacy-alias) methods that are NOT in the contract and not in GATEWAY_RPC_LEGACY_METHOD_ALIASES: health (3819), frames.list (3921), frames.get (3934), attempts.list (3952), attempts.get (3968), getDevToolsSnapshot (4017), runs.diff (4423), runs.rerun (4552). These are dispatched and live but have no published request/response schema, example, error set, or maturity. getRequiredScopeForGatewayMethod (rpc/index.ts:725-748) hard-codes scopes for them, confirming they're real, scoped methods deliberately kept out of the frozen contract (so this is contract-completeness drift, not an auth hole). The audit's '10' is approximate; at least 8 dispatched methods are uncontracted.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add these live methods to GATEWAY_RPC_DEFINITIONS (or an explicit 'internal/legacy' contract section) with request/response schemas, so the canonical contract and the rpc-contract test cover every dispatched method instead of only the 19 stable ones.

stillOpen.finding: P1 OpenAPI non-JSON request bodies (multipart/form-data, x-www-form-urlencoded) are silently dropped
stillOpen.reason: buildOperationSchema only reads requestBody.content['application/json'] (buildOperationSchema.js:45) — any operation whose body is multipart/form-data or x-www-form-urlencoded gets no `body` field in the generated schema. And executeRequest always serializes args.body with JSON.stringify and forces Content-Type: application/json (_helpers.js:121-125). So non-JSON request bodies are never modeled and never sent in the correct encoding.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Extend buildOperationSchema to fall back to multipart/form-data and application/x-www-form-urlencoded content schemas, and in executeRequest encode the body (FormData / URLSearchParams) with the matching Content-Type based on the operation's chosen media type.

stillOpen.finding: P2 Scorer execution never persists context/groundTruth even when received, and inputJson is the only correlation column
stillOpen.reason: ScorerContext (types.ts:108-117) lacks context and groundTruth fields, so runSingleScorerEffect (run-scorers.js:66-71) passes only {input, output, latencyMs, outputSchema} to scorer.score() — faithfulness/groundTruth scorers always see context/groundTruth as undefined when driven by the engine. The persisted row (run-scorers.js:112-129) and ScoreRow type (types.ts:72-89) have only inputJson/outputJson — no contextJson/groundTruthJson columns — so context/groundTruth are never stored, and inputJson is the only correlation key.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add context/groundTruth to ScorerContext and forward them into scorer.score(); add contextJson/groundTruthJson columns to the scorer-results schema + ScoreRow and persist them in the inserted row.

resolved.finding: Hallucinated model ID 'claude-sonnet-4-7' in examples/ralph-loop.jsx
resolved.evidence: examples/ralph-loop.jsx:9 now reads model: "claude-sonnet-4-6" (a real active model per the claude-api catalog). A regression test apps/cli/tests/examples-models.test.js denylists 'claude-sonnet-4-7' and asserts no example references it; the test passes (green).

resolved.finding: checkUsageLimit ignores `period` — control-plane/src/index.js
resolved.evidence: checkUsageLimit now computes periodMs = USAGE_LIMIT_PERIODS.get(period) and sinceMs = max(0, untilMs - periodMs), and the usage SUM SQL filters observed_at_ms >= sinceMs AND <= untilMs. Window is period-aware. Tested in control-plane.test.js 'usage limit periods are validated and define default quota windows'.

resolved.finding: Unguarded JSON.parse on DB-stored metadata_json — control-plane/src/index.js:288
resolved.evidence: The only JSON.parse (index.js:295) is inside parseJsonObject(), wrapped in try/catch that warns and returns {} on SyntaxError. All metadata reads use parseJsonObject(row.metadataJson). Tested by control-plane.test.js 'audit export defaults malformed stored metadata to empty objects'.

resolved.finding: smithers tree --watch crashes on first delta (CLI passed DevToolsNode to applyDelta) — apps/cli/src/tree.js:240-241
resolved.evidence: tree.js assigns snapshot = event.snapshot (a DevToolsSnapshotV1) and calls applyDelta(snapshot, delta); applyDelta's contract takes DevToolsSnapshotV1 and reads snapshot.seq/root. Regression test apps/cli/tests/tree.test.js:251 '--watch applies the first delta to the full snapshot without crashing' asserts no InvalidDelta and that the delta is applied (seq 0 then 1).

resolved.finding: Published TaskDescriptor type is missing forkSource — packages/graph/src/index.d.ts
resolved.evidence: packages/graph/src/index.d.ts:132 now includes `forkSource?: string;` with the matching doc comment, identical to packages/graph/src/types.ts:154. Type drift is gone.

resolved.finding: tsconfig path map for the protocol root points at a non-existent file (src/index.js) — tsconfig.json:162-164
resolved.evidence: packages/protocol/src/index.js now exists (201 bytes, real exports). Both tsconfig.json:163 and examples/tsconfig.json:118 point @smithers-orchestrator/protocol -> packages/protocol/src/index.js, which resolves.

resolved.finding: fiberToNode in SmithersDevTools only descends one level through non-Smithers fibers — packages/react-reconciler/src/devtools/SmithersDevTools.js:137-152
resolved.evidence: collectSmithersDescendants recurses: when fiberToNode(child) returns null (non-Smithers fiber) it calls collectSmithersDescendants(child.child, ...) to descend deeper, so arbitrarily-deep Smithers nodes are captured. Regression test packages/react-reconciler/tests/devtools-deep-wrapper.test.js:60 'captures Smithers nodes nested more than one non-Smithers fiber deep' passes.

resolved.finding: runs.rerun uses backend-specific raw SQL with a hardcoded `payload` column — packages/server/src/gateway.js:4542-4546
resolved.evidence: runs.rerun now resolves the workflow's input table via resolveSchema and loads the original input through the backend-agnostic loadInput() (from @smithers-orchestrator/db/snapshot, which has Postgres + drizzle paths against the actual input table), then routes to runs.create. No hardcoded `payload`-column raw SQL remains. Tested by gateway.test.jsx 'reruns with the original schema-backed input row' and the hijack+rerun test.

resolved.finding: Bare require() inside an ESM module breaks workspaceAdd pre-create cleanup — packages/vcs/src/jj.js:197-211
resolved.evidence: jj.js imports fs via ESM `import * as fs from "node:fs"` (line 17) and workspaceAdd's pre-create cleanup uses fs.existsSync/fs.rmSync from that import. `grep -c 'require(' packages/vcs/src/jj.js` returns 0 — no bare require remains, consistent with resolveJjBinary's ESM import.

resolved.finding: enum is ignored for numbers/integers and for typeless schemas — packages/openapi/src/jsonSchemaToZod.js:91-94,115-124,78-80
resolved.evidence: buildNumber now applies enum via z.intersection(num, buildLiteralUnion(s.enum)) for number/integer, and the typeless fallback (jsonSchemaToZod ~line 78) returns buildLiteralUnion(s.enum) for typeless enum. Tested in schema-conversion.test.js 'converts number and integer enums' (asserts parse(3)/parse(1.5) throw) and 'converts typeless enum'.

stillOpen.finding: `snapshots --json` emits unparseable stdout (human text + framework CTA injection)
stillOpen.file: apps/cli/src/index.js:5018-5042 (snapshots command); DEVTOOLS_COMMANDS set at apps/cli/src/index.js:2638
stillOpen.reason: Reproduced live: `smithers snapshots <runId> --json` prints 'No durability snapshots for run ...' then a JSON CTA ('Skills are out of date'). 'snapshots' is NOT in DEVTOOLS_COMMANDS = new Set(["tree","diff","output","rewind"]), so rewriteDevtoolsJsonFlagArgv never maps --json to the command's -j option (json stays false) and the global Incur --json triggers CTA injection. stdout is unparseable.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add 'snapshots' to DEVTOOLS_COMMANDS (and argvRequestsJsonMode) so --json maps to the command option and suppresses framework CTA.

stillOpen.finding: `timeline --json` emits unparseable stdout (human TUI text + framework CTA injection)
stillOpen.reason: Reproduced live: `smithers timeline <runId> --json` prints 'json-run' (formatTimelineForTui output, because c.options.json was never set) then returns c.ok(...) which the framework augments with a 'Skills are out of date' CTA JSON. 'timeline' is not in DEVTOOLS_COMMANDS so --json is not rewritten to the command option. stdout is unparseable.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Route timeline --json through the devtools json-flag rewrite (or a raw-json handler) so it emits only the timeline JSON and no CTA.

stillOpen.finding: smithers cron scheduler spawns `bun run src/index.js` which does not exist in user projects
stillOpen.reason: processCronEffect still does spawn("bun", ["run", "src/index.js", "up", job.workflowPath, "-d"], { cwd: process.cwd() }) — assumes a src/index.js relative to the user's CWD, which does not exist in installed projects.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Spawn the resolved smithers CLI bin (e.g. process.execPath + the CLI entry, or `smithers up`) instead of a hardcoded `bun run src/index.js`.

stillOpen.finding: apps/review CLI --publish defaults to the non-functional review.smithers.sh domain
stillOpen.reason: DEFAULT_PUBLISH_URL = "https://review.smithers.sh" is still the hardcoded default when no env var / config override is set. Whether the domain is actually live could not be verified externally (unverified - needs manual check of the domain), but the code default is unchanged from the audited state.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Verify the domain is live; if not, require an explicit --publish-url / config and fail clearly rather than defaulting to a dead host.

stillOpen.finding: SSE metering silently under-bills responses larger than 1MB
stillOpen.reason: teeForMetering caps accumulation at cap = 1 << 20 (1MB): `if (acc.length >= cap) continue;`. The Anthropic SSE usage arrives in the final message_delta near the END of the stream, so a >1MB response drops the usage chunk and under-bills. No test covers the cap.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Parse usage incrementally as SSE frames arrive (or specifically retain message_delta usage) instead of truncating the accumulated buffer at 1MB.

stillOpen.finding: Session spend-cap enforcement races under concurrent requests
stillOpen.reason: spentUsd is read from auth.spentUsd at request start and checked `if (spentUsd >= spendCapUsd) return 402`; the actual increment happens later via recordUsage in deps.waitUntil after the stream. Two concurrent requests both read the same pre-spend value, both pass, both record. No atomic check-and-increment. Only the sequential 'already over cap' case is tested.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Make the cap check and spend increment atomic (single conditional UPDATE / transaction) before forwarding, not a read-then-act with deferred recordUsage.

stillOpen.finding: parseAccountsFile does not enforce the documented configDir XOR apiKey invariant (secrets can leak into subscription records)
stillOpen.reason: Validation only checks subscription providers have configDir (line 79) and api-key providers have apiKey (line 82); it never rejects an entry carrying BOTH. Lines 88-89 unconditionally copy configDir AND apiKey when present, so a subscription account with a stray apiKey keeps it. No test enforces XOR (accounts.test.js only checks presence).
stillOpen.fixSketch: Throw ACCOUNTS_FILE_INVALID when a subscription account carries apiKey or an api-key account carries configDir; only copy the field appropriate to the provider class.

stillOpen.finding: agents codegen also fails the configDir-XOR-apiKey invariant, propagating a stray API key into spawned agents
stillOpen.reason: renderAccountProviderLine pushes opts independently: `if (account.configDir) opts.push(configDir...)` AND `if (account.apiKey) opts.push(apiKey...)`. If an account carries both (allowed by parseAccountsFile), both are emitted into the generated `new SmithersXAgent({...})`, leaking the apiKey into the spawned agent.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Emit configDir XOR apiKey based on provider class, mirroring the parser fix; never emit both.

stillOpen.finding: SuperSmithers collides all 4 task outputs onto one key when reportOutput is provided
stillOpen.reason: When reportOutput is set, readOutput (39), proposeOutput (56), applyOutput (69) and finalOutput (86) all equal reportOutput, so the read/propose/apply/report tasks write to the same output table and overwrite each other — only the last survives.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Only the final report task should use reportOutput; keep distinct per-task defaults (or derive `${prefix}-read/-propose/-apply` keys) for the intermediate tasks.

stillOpen.finding: answerHumanRequest argument order differs between SmithersDb and InMemoryStorage
stillOpen.reason: SmithersDb.answerHumanRequest(requestId, responseJson, answeredAtMs, answeredBy); InMemoryStorage.answerHumanRequest(requestId, responseJson, answeredBy=null, answeredAtMs=Date.now()); StorageServiceShape.ts:86 declares (requestId, responseJson, answeredBy?, answeredAtMs?). 3rd/4th args are swapped between implementations. InMemoryStorage is effectively dead (no real wiring) but the interface contract still diverges.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Align the InMemoryStorage signature and StorageServiceShape to the SmithersDb (answeredAtMs then answeredBy) ordering, or delete InMemoryStorage if truly dead.

stillOpen.finding: applyDelta validation is asymmetric: addNode/updateProps/updateTask accept malformed payloads
stillOpen.reason: replaceRoot validates `op.node` is an object, but addNode does `cloneValue(op.node)` with no guard, updateProps does `target.node.props = cloneValue(op.props)` with no guard, and updateTask doesn't validate op.task. Malformed payloads corrupt the tree. Tests cover only valid payloads + replaceRoot malformed.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Validate op.node (addNode) and op.props (updateProps) are objects, throwing InvalidDeltaError on malformed input, mirroring replaceRoot.

stillOpen.finding: applyDelta updateProps with no props key sets node.props = undefined
stillOpen.reason: updateProps does `target.node.props = cloneValue(op.props)`; when op.props is missing this sets props to undefined with no validation/rejection.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Reject updateProps ops whose props is not an object (throw InvalidDeltaError).

stillOpen.finding: snapshotSerializer maxEntries does not bound flat arrays/objects of scalars
stillOpen.reason: Scalars (string/number/boolean) return early (before the `state.traversed >= state.maxEntries` check and before traversed++ at line ~78). Only container objects/arrays increment traversed, so a flat array/object of millions of scalar leaves is never bounded by maxEntries.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Increment/check traversed per emitted entry including scalar array/object members, or cap array length and object key count explicitly.

stillOpen.finding: builder fragment(_inputSchema) silently discards its input schema argument
stillOpen.reason: fragment(_inputSchema) { return makeFactory(); } — the schema arg (underscore-prefixed, unused) is dropped. By contrast workflow(options) threads options.input through decodeSchema/encodeSchema for validation, so a fragment's declared input schema never validates input.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Thread the fragment's input schema into the factory/compiled fragment so it validates input on mount, or remove the parameter if intentionally unsupported.

stillOpen.finding: Committed generated index.d.ts is stale — public error-code types missing 6 real codes
stillOpen.reason: The runtime catalog smithersErrorDefinitions.js has 96 codes; the index.d.ts namespace declares only 90. Diff shows exactly 6 missing: AGENT_CONFIG_INVALID, TASK_FORK_CYCLE, TASK_FORK_SESSION_UNAVAILABLE, TASK_FORK_SOURCE_NOT_COMPLETE, TASK_FORK_SOURCE_NOT_FOUND, TASK_HIJACK_UNSUPPORTED. KnownSmithersErrorCode = keyof typeof smithersErrorDefinitions therefore omits them from published types.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Regenerate packages/errors/src/index.d.ts from the current runtime catalog (fix the dts generation) so all 96 codes are present.

stillOpen.finding: tsup bundled-declaration generation drops error codes; published types diverge from runtime with no CI guard
stillOpen.reason: index.d.ts is still missing 6 codes (proving tsup dts generation still drops them). check-docs.mjs guards docs/reference/types.mdx against the runtime catalog (checkKnownErrorCodeUnion) and that doc lists all 96, so CI stays green — but nothing guards the published packages/errors/src/index.d.ts, so the type/runtime divergence is unguarded.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add a check that the generated packages/errors/src/index.d.ts error-code set matches smithersErrorDefinitions.js (extend check-docs/check-llms), and fix the generator.

stillOpen.finding: isSmithersError returns true for EngineError, so toSmithersError(engineError) returns a non-SmithersError (violates @returns {SmithersError})
stillOpen.reason: Reproduced: isSmithersError(new EngineError({code:'SCHEDULER_ERROR',...})) === true (it has a known code + message), so isSmithersErrorLike is true, and toSmithersError(engineError) with no label/code/details hits the early return (line 44) and returns the raw EngineError — `wrapped instanceof SmithersError === false`, `wrapped === cause`. The existing test only covers the labeled (wrapping) path.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Make isSmithersError exclude EngineError (require SmithersError-brand or instanceof), and/or have toSmithersError always wrap EngineError into a real SmithersError.

stillOpen.finding: errorToJson loses code/context for EngineError (fromTaggedError can't map it)
stillOpen.reason: Reproduced: errorToJson(engineError) returns only {name, message, stack} — code and context dropped. fromTaggedError switches on _tag and has no 'EngineError' case (default returns undefined), so errorToJson falls through to the instanceof Error branch.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add an EngineError branch to errorToJson (emit code + context) and/or a fromTaggedError case mapping _tag 'EngineError' to a SmithersError.

stillOpen.finding: streamRunEventsResilient replay detection reads frame.payload.event, but real gap_resync frames have no payload.event
stillOpen.reason: Server (gateway.js sendEvent at 3312 + sendRunGapResync at 1811) puts the event name in frame.event; the gap_resync payload is {apiVersion,type:'GapResync',streamId,runId,fromSeq,toSeq,snapshot} with NO `event` field. Line 384 checks frame.payload.event === 'run.gap_resync', always false for real frames, so isReplay never true and backoff resets on a bare replay frame at connection start (the exact bug the resilient stream tries to avoid). The passing test gatewayReconnectResume.test.ts uses a fixture (sendGapResync) that fabricates payload.event, masking the bug.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Use frame.event === 'run.gap_resync' (and frame.event === 'run.completed') as in streamRunEvents line 324; fix the test fixture to match the real server frame shape.

stillOpen.finding: useGatewayExtensionStream never clears `error` after a successful reconnect
stillOpen.reason: setError(undefined) only runs once at effect start (line 63). On reconnect the success path sets attempt=0/latest/frames but never clears error. Confirmed by extension-hooks.test.ts:295 which asserts snapshot.error stays === firstError even after a healthy reconnect delivers a new frame.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Call setError(undefined) on the first successful frame after a reconnect (in the for-await loop body).

stillOpen.finding: buildRunTree recursion is unguarded against cyclic childIds
stillOpen.reason: build(node) maps node.childIds -> byId.get -> build recursively with no visited set; cyclic childIds (A->B->A) cause infinite recursion / stack overflow.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Track a visited Set of node ids and skip already-visited children during the build recursion.

stillOpen.finding: flattenGatewayRunNode visit() is unguarded against cycles
stillOpen.reason: visit(node) recurses through node.children with no visited set; a cyclic children graph causes infinite recursion.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add a visited Set keyed by node.id and skip nodes already visited.

stillOpen.finding: deleteThread is non-transactional across two writes (partial-delete risk)
stillOpen.reason: deleteThreadEffect issues two separate writeEffect deletes (messages, then thread) with no transaction. If the second fails after the first succeeds, the thread row remains while its messages are gone (partial delete).
stillOpen.fixSketch: Wrap both deletes in a single DB transaction (adapter.transaction / db.transaction).

stillOpen.finding: additionalProperties as a SchemaObject/RefObject is silently dropped (typed extra props not validated)
stillOpen.reason: buildObject only handles additionalProperties === true || undefined (catchall(z.unknown())). When additionalProperties is a schema object (e.g. {type:'string'}), neither branch runs, so the object is built with no catchall and the typed extra props are dropped/unvalidated. No test covers additionalProperties-as-schema.
stillOpen.fixSketch: When additionalProperties is a SchemaObject/RefObject, set obj.catchall(jsonSchemaToZod(additionalProperties, spec, visited)).

stillOpen.finding: Pre-loaded object specs without an `openapi` key (e.g. Swagger 2.0) throw a misleading parse error
stillOpen.reason: Both guard pre-loaded objects with `'openapi' in input`. A Swagger 2.0 object has `swagger` not `openapi`, so it falls through to treating the object as a string path (readFileSync(object)) producing a misleading error.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Accept any non-null object spec (or detect `swagger`/`openapi`) and surface a clear 'unsupported spec version' error for Swagger 2.0.

stillOpen.finding: Repeated path parameters only substitute the first occurrence
stillOpen.reason: buildUrl uses url.replace(`{${key}}`, ...) which replaces only the first occurrence; a path like /{id}/sub/{id} leaves later occurrences unsubstituted.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Use url.replaceAll(`{${key}}`, ...) (or a global regex) so all occurrences are substituted.

stillOpen.finding: A parameter literally named `body` is silently overwritten by the request body
stillOpen.reason: Parameters are written to props[param.name] (so props.body for a param named 'body'), then the request body unconditionally sets props.body = bodySchema, overwriting the parameter with no collision detection.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Detect a 'body' name collision and namespace one side (e.g. requestBody under a non-colliding key, or rename the conflicting parameter).

stillOpen.finding: pi-plugin approval detection ignores state-string normalization, so /smithers-approve and the active-run prompt miss waiting nodes
stillOpen.reason: Both use raw equality (node.state === 'waiting-approval'). The codebase has normalizeState (DevToolsStore.ts:103 / RunTree.ts:28) which lowercases/trims and maps _/whitespace to '-' and also treats 'blocked'/'waitingapproval' as waiting-approval; engine/approvals.js:45 confirms both 'waiting-approval' and 'waiting_approval' occur. The raw comparisons miss any non-canonical form.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Use the existing normalizeState() in both approval filters (extension.ts:502 and buildSmithersPiSystemPrompt.ts:107).

stillOpen.finding: RunInspector subscribes to the store but never unsubscribes; dispose() is dead code
stillOpen.reason: Constructor calls store.subscribe(() => this.invalidate()) and discards the returned unsubscribe fn (DevToolsStore.subscribe returns () => listeners.delete(listener)). dispose() only calls store.disconnect(), which does not clear listeners (it even emit()s to them). The invalidate listener leaks per inspector.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Capture the unsubscribe returned by store.subscribe and call it in dispose().

stillOpen.finding: reconciler.js has top-level side effects but package.json declares sideEffects:false
stillOpen.reason: Module top-level installs the RDT hook (if (!hookHost.__REACT_DEVTOOLS_GLOBAL_HOOK__) installRDTHook()) and calls reconciler.injectIntoDevTools({...}) — real side effects. package.json:6 declares "sideEffects": false, so a bundler may tree-shake and skip them.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Move the side-effectful devtools injection behind an explicit init function, or mark the file in package.json sideEffects.

stillOpen.finding: Container stores a single root; top-level Fragment/array silently truncated to last child
stillOpen.reason: appendChildToContainer(container, child) does container.root = child. Multiple top-level children (a Fragment/array) each overwrite container.root, keeping only the last.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Support multiple container children (array root) or throw on >1 top-level child instead of silently truncating.

stillOpen.finding: insertInContainerBefore ignores its _beforeChild argument, silently dropping ordering semantics
stillOpen.reason: insertInContainerBefore(container, child, _beforeChild) { container.root = child; } — ignores _beforeChild and overwrites the single root, dropping insertion ordering.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Implement ordered insertion against a multi-child container root that honors _beforeChild.

stillOpen.finding: Deep subpath imports resolve types to index.d.ts and break strict TS consumers (TS2305/TS2459)
stillOpen.reason: The './*' export maps types to ./src/index.d.ts for ALL subpaths, and only index.d.ts exists (no per-file .d.ts). External strict TS consumers importing e.g. @smithers-orchestrator/sandbox/SandboxHandle get the root barrel types, not the subpath's types. Internally masked only by the tsconfig path map (tsconfig.json:194-196).
stillOpen.fixSketch: Emit per-subpath declaration files (tsup dts per entry) and map './*' types to ./src/*.d.ts.

stillOpen.finding: Type-only subpath @smithers-orchestrator/sandbox/SandboxHandle also breaks external strict TS consumers
stillOpen.reason: Same root cause as the deep-subpath finding: stallSandbox.ts:2 does `import type { SandboxHandle } from "@smithers-orchestrator/sandbox/SandboxHandle"`, but the published exports './*' types resolve to index.d.ts; with no SandboxHandle.d.ts an external strict consumer cannot resolve the type.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Same fix as the deep-subpath finding — per-subpath .d.ts so SandboxHandle resolves from its own subpath.

stillOpen.finding: WorkflowSessionLive builds a single shared session (latent correctness bug if consumed)
stillOpen.reason: WorkflowSessionLive = Layer.sync(WorkflowSession, makeWorkflowSession). makeWorkflowSession closes over a single mutable `state` (runId, Maps/Sets for descriptors/states/outputs/etc). Layer.sync builds it once and shares it, so multiple runs consuming this layer would share one session's mutable state.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Use Layer.effect/scoped that builds a fresh makeWorkflowSession per scope, or don't expose a shared-instance layer.

stillOpen.finding: context and groundTruth never reach scorers in real execution — faithfulnessScorer silently broken on live/batch runs
stillOpen.reason: scorer.score is called with only {input, output, latencyMs, outputSchema}; ScorerContext (types.ts:108-117) has no context/groundTruth fields; engine.js:4206-4211 passes none. faithfulnessScorer.js:26 reads `context` from its score input, so it always sees 'No context provided' on live/batch runs. ScorerInput (index.d.ts:26-28) declares groundTruth?/context? but they are never threaded.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add context/groundTruth to ScorerContext + the engine's runScorersAsync call and pass them into scorer.score in run-scorers.js.

stillOpen.finding: aggregateScores builds SQL via string interpolation with a one-character escaper
stillOpen.reason: Conditions are interpolated as run_id = '<escapeSql(value)>'; escapeSql only doubles single quotes (.replace(/'/g, "''")). Still string-built SQL rather than parameterized; fragile across backends (e.g. backslash handling).
stillOpen.fixSketch: Use parameterized queries (adapter.rawQuery with bound params) instead of interpolated WHERE clauses.

stillOpen.finding: diffSnapshots ignores outputTable changes in node-change detection
stillOpen.reason: Node change detection compares only aNode.state, lastAttempt, label; it does not compare outputTable, even though NodeSnapshot (NodeSnapshot.ts:6) carries outputTable. A node whose outputTable changed (with other fields equal) is not reported as changed.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Add aNode.outputTable !== bNode.outputTable to the node-change condition.

stillOpen.finding: readUsageCache does not validate cache version and accepts array `entries`
stillOpen.reason: It returns {version:1, entries: parsed.entries} whenever `parsed.entries && typeof parsed.entries === 'object'` — arrays pass (typeof [] === 'object'), and parsed.version is never validated.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Reject when parsed.version !== 1 and require !Array.isArray(parsed.entries) (plain object) before accepting entries.

stillOpen.finding: Anthropic/OpenAI count windows can report a negative `used` when remaining > limit
stillOpen.reason: Both compute used = limit - remaining with no clamp; if a stale/inconsistent header has remaining > limit, used is negative.
stillOpen.fixSketch: Clamp used = Math.max(0, limit - remaining) in both parsers.

stillOpen.finding: Extension-stream reconnect backoff timer ignores the abort signal
stillOpen.reason: The reconnect wait is `await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, gatewayBackoffDelay(...)))` with no abort listener; on unmount/abort during the wait the timer still runs to completion (cleanup is only caught on the next loop iteration), and the timer is never cleared.
